campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also covered council reports and welcomed public input on various matters, including an upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on council reports
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council approved a resolution to identify certain properties and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice concerns and the council will make a final decision on the matter.
keyTopics: Resolution passed to identify specific properties in Saratoga
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests and comments before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a new youth commissioner, and call for a municipal election in November. The council also authorized several agreements for town services and approved a new residential development map.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election scheduled
New youth commissioner confirmed by selection committee
Tract 10675 Boulevard development map approved
Five-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. authorized
Building division plan check agreements authorized

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for June 16 and June 23, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to each agenda, set time limits for discussions when needed, and worked on procedural requirements. They also discussed the Community and Economic Development Committee's work plan.
keyTopics: Adding and organizing items for the June 16 Final Agenda
Planning the June 23 Draft Agenda
Setting time limits for agenda discussions
Community and Economic Development Committee Work Plan

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's city council met on June 9, 2026 to handle personnel matters in a closed session and then celebrate two important observances. The council discussed the appointment of a new City Attorney in private, then moved to public session to formally recognize Pride Month and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: City Attorney appointment (discussed in closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 16, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two meetings on June 16, 2026: a closed-door session (details not disclosed to the public) followed by a study session where council members discussed a Community Equity Assessment to evaluate fairness and inclusion efforts across city services.
keyTopics: Community Equity Assessment update and findings
Closed session discussion (specific topics not publicly disclosed)
City equity and inclusion initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to make sure local rules align with state law protections. The meeting also included a public comment period for residents to raise any non-agenda items with the council.
keyTopics: Review of existing zoning for parks and recreational facilities
Corrections to parkland site designations
Ensuring compliance with state law protections for recreational areas
Public comment period on non-agenda matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 9, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 9, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed both in-person attendance and remote participation via Zoom. The city provided multiple ways for residents to join and submit comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available in-person and online via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents unable to attend in person
Written public comment submission through eComments system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 16, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's spending plan for 2026-2027, including federal community development money, and to greenlight a new residential development project. The council also discussed a new crime prevention program focused on storefronts.
keyTopics: 2026-2027 budget and Community Development Block Grant funding
New residential development permit and site plan approval
Storefront Crime Prevention Program
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 12, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss plans for getting more electric vehicles charged around town. They reviewed design guidelines for a new public EV charging hub program and talked about strategies to help the whole community switch to electric power.
keyTopics: Design guidelines for public EV charging stations
Community-wide electrification strategies
Environmental review exemption for projects matching the city's general plan
